results.title: 😺 ChatGPT in your pocket | Product Hunt
results.content:
Power users will be happy to hear that the mobile app syncs your history across your devices, meaning it can pull the previous conversations you’ve had with the web client and make them accessible – on the go.

Siri has a challenger: With Apple’s seemingly radio silence and lack of progress on the AI front, coupled with Siri’s existing issues, the new ChatGPT presents an interesting future where we might see iPhone users opting to replace Apple’s voice assistant with ChatGPT. [...] Newsletter Sponsor

They kept using words like colleague, coworker, team member. One CEO called it the glue holding their e-commerce business together, which is a lot, but also… you see why.
It lives in Slack and plugs into 3,000+ tools, so instead of jumping between tabs, you just ask for the thing. Pull Stripe against HubSpot, check Sentry alerts, spin up a campaign brief, build a landing page, send a report upstairs. It all happens there.

One user said it was the first time AI felt like a real coworker, which is either exciting or slightly concerning depending on your week.

The Game Wallet allows you to transform your Gameboy into a crypto wallet.

Twitter Race gamifies your Twitter growth by pitting you against a friend in a growth race. [...] As mentioned above, “hey ChatGPT” doesn’t have the same ring to it, but given the feature set the app comes with, combined with the household name it boasts, it wouldn’t be too surprising if it gave Siri a run for its money. Who knows, it could bolster Apple to double down on its AI efforts to wade off the challenger. Win-win for us, the consumer.

If you want to try it out, the ChatGPT mobile app is currently only available in the US but will be expanding shortly, and an Android app is on the horizon.

## We asked 34 customers what Viktor does for them. Not one said chatbot.

Newsletter Sponsor

results.title: Best AI Automation Tools: Nominate Your Favorites for the Product ...
results.content:
Hovhannes Ghevondyan
Aikido Security

Aikido Security

I really like the idea of @Trace (even though it's kinda rough on implementation side rn). I gave it a messy idea and it came back with a workflow that usually makes sense. Some tasks it flagged as automatable that I hadn’t even thought about. Also nice that I can flip a task back to myself when the AI struggles. Overall really cool idea and I hope it continues to improve!

Christophe HK
Haz Hubble
Pally - AI Relationship Management

Pally - AI Relationship Management

@Trace Very solid idea! Takes some time playing with the tool to get the results, but when it works it's very nice!

Nodari Kolmakhidze [...] This one is for the tools that actually do work for you – clearing chores, running workflows in the background, or quietly taking over a chunk of your week without turning into another dashboard you have to babysit.

We’re digging into the AI Automation category based on traction and reviews, not hype. So we’d love your help:

Drop your favorite AI automation tools in the thread

Add a quick note on what they actually do for you day to day

And if you want them in the running for an Orbit, leave a review on their Product Hunt Hub

Think of this as nominations-by-use-case, not just vibes. Which tools have actually earned a permanent spot in your stack?

55

356

Add a comment

### Replies

Aleksandar Blazhev
ZeroHuman.

ZeroHuman.

Thank you for the thread @aaronoleary: [...] n&n — Their template library and now ability to prompt to build workflows will help a lot of folks automate their tasks.

Zapier — The OG of automation now with AI capabilities. I hunted two launches for them this year for their MCP.

IFTTT — Good for simple automation tasks and has a mobile app too.

Trace — Been using since its release. I usually just dump a rough prompt in and it builds a full workflow for me, which is honestly quite cool. I like that I can tweak the graph after if something feels off. Switching tasks between human and agents is super handy when the AI isn’t quite there yet. Would love better notifications when something fails, but overall it’s been alright.

ZeroGPU

#### What's great

As a founder, this is exactly the kind of product I’ve needed.

Document signing is one of those workflows that should be simple, fast, and trustworthy but too often it feels locked inside expensive, rigid tools. Documenso feels like a much-needed rethink: open source, clean to use, flexible enough to self-host or use hosted, and built with APIs, templates, direct links, and team workflows in mind.
